body:
### Describe the bug

```sh
Error compiling Cython file:
------------------------------------------------------------
...
ctypedef fused numT:
    int32_t
    int64_t

cdef void func(numT dummy):
    foo[numT](dummy)
              ^
------------------------------------------------------------
fused.pyx:19:14: Cannot coerce to a type that is not specialized

Error compiling Cython file:
------------------------------------------------------------
...
ctypedef fused numT:
    int32_t
    int64_t

cdef void func(numT dummy):
    foo[numT](dummy)
              ^
------------------------------------------------------------
fused.pyx:19:14: Cannot coerce to a type that is not specialized

Error compiling Cython file:
------------------------------------------------------------
...
ctypedef fused numT:
    int32_t
    int64_t

cdef void func(numT dummy):
    foo[numT](dummy)
       ^
------------------------------------------------------------
fused.pyx:19:7: Invalid use of fused types, type cannot be specialized

Error compiling Cython file:
------------------------------------------------------------
...
ctypedef fused numT:
    int32_t
    int64_t

cdef void func(numT dummy):
    foo[numT](dummy)
       ^
------------------------------------------------------------
fused.pyx:19:7: Invalid use of fused types, type cannot be specialized
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/jfaibussowit/soft/packages/base_venv/bin/cythonize", line 7, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
             ~~~~^^
  File "/Users/jfaibussowit/soft/packages/base_venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/Cython/Build/Cythonize.py", line 324, in main
    ext_modules_by_basedir = _cython_compile_files(all_paths, options)
  File "/Users/jfaibussowit/soft/packages/base_venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/Cython/Build/Cythonize.py", line 51, in _cython_compile_files
    ext_modules = cythonize(
        paths,
    ...<8 lines>...
        language=options.language,
        **options.options)
  File "/Users/jfaibussowit/soft/packages/base_venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/Cython/Build/Dependencies.py", line 1153, in cythonize
    cythonize_one(*args)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^
  File "/Users/jfaibussowit/soft/packages/base_venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/Cython/Build/Dependencies.py", line 1297, in cythonize_one
    raise CompileError(None, pyx_file)
Cython.Compiler.Errors.CompileError: /Users/jfaibussowit/soft/scratch/fused.pyx
```

### Code to reproduce the behaviour:

```cython
# distutils: language = c++

from libc.stdint cimport int32_t, int64_t

cdef extern from *:
  """
  template <typename T>
  void foo(T)
  {
  }
  """
  void foo[T](T)

ctypedef fused numT:
    int32_t
    int64_t

cdef void func(numT dummy):
    foo[numT](dummy)   # !!

cdef int32_t INT32_T = -1

def test1():
    return func(INT32_T)
```
Note that if you replace the `foo[numT](dummy)` call with
```python
foo(dummy)
```
it successfully compiles.

### Expected behaviour

Should pass either `int32_t` or `int64_t` as template parameter.

### OS

macOS

### Python version

3.14.3

### Cython version

3.2.4

### Additional context

_No response_

comment_data.body:
You can use `cython.typeof`. This doesn't seem documented but it generates the correct code.

```python
cimport cython

...

cdef void func(numT dummy):
    foo[cython.typeof(dummy)](dummy)   # !!
```

I think this is a consequence of some string literal to type conversion, similar to how you can index "def" fused functions with a string to select the fused type from Cython and it'll figure it out statically.

title: [BUG] Fused types cannot be used as C++ template parameter
